steps.title: Use axis of symmetry for the parabola
steps.content: For any parabola, the x-intercepts are symmetric about the vertical line through the vertex. Since the vertex is at $(-3, 6)$, the axis of symmetry is the line $x = -3$.

steps.title: Apply the symmetry formula
steps.content:
If one x-intercept is at $x = r_1$, the other is at $x = 2h - r_1$, where $h = -3$ is the x-coordinate of the vertex. Substitute $r_1 = -\frac{17}{4}$:
$$x = 2(-3) - \left(-\frac{17}{4}\right) = -6 + \frac{17}{4}$$
Convert to a common denominator:
$$x = -\frac{24}{4} + \frac{17}{4} = -\frac{7}{4}$$
The other x-intercept is $\left(-\frac{7}{4}, 0\right)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
